title: Investigate an unexpected replace in a plan
description: When plan shows destroy-and-recreate, find the forcing attribute and decide whether the replacement is acceptable.
body: Steps: 1. In the plan, locate the resource marked '-/+ destroy and then create replacement'. 2. Read the attribute annotated '# forces replacement'. 3. Determine if changing that attribute in place is possible (lifecycle, separate resource) or if recreation is inherent. 4. Assess data loss / downtime: will the recreate drop a disk, IP, or stateful store? 5. Decide to proceed, use create_before_destroy, or avoid the change. Rollback point: before apply, while it is still only a plan. Validation: the forcing attribute and blast radius are understood and documented; an unexplained replacement of a database or volume must be escalated, not applied.
